summary.intent: Sync a TagGroup component spec file with an implementation change that allows disabled items to emit Focus/Blur events, then push and restart a Codex review loop.
summary.outcome: The spec code example was patched to reflect that disabled Focus/Blur transitions are observable, validated via cargo xtask, committed as a follow-up, and pushed to the PR branch.

repo: Spec files live under spec/components/data-display/ and contain embedded code snippets that are independently compiled and validated via `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets` and `cargo xtask spec validate`.
The project uses a Codex review loop triggered after each push; inline PR review threads must be replied to and resolved manually before retriggering.
Disabled state in TagGroup previously silently dropped Focus/Blur events; the updated contract makes disabled focus observable (state is recorded) while keeping mutation/navigation events as no-ops.

code.finding: Disabled branch spec example did not record focused state despite prose saying Focus/Blur are allowed for AT; patched to match implementation.
Broader disabled section reviewed to ensure the narrow fix didn't conflict with existing no-op behavior for mutation/navigation events.

workflow: Spec snippets are part of the contract and validated independently of Rust crate tests — always run `cargo xtask spec validate` after editing spec markdown.
Separating the review fix commit from the spec sync commit keeps PR history legible and makes each change's purpose clear.
Inline review thread anchors become outdated after a force-push or rebase, which is expected behavior and not a blocker.

friction: The user had to prompt the assistant to check whether the spec had been updated — the spec sync was not part of the original fix scope and was nearly missed.
Inline comment anchors went stale after the push, requiring re-fetching thread identifiers before replying.
open_items: Codex review re-pass not yet confirmed complete at transcript end — outcome of the retriggered review is unknown.

Proptest state machine file (crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/data_display/tag_group.rs) was diffed but no changes to it were mentioned — may need updating to cover the new disabled focus behavior.
